---
title: "RL Fundamentals"
---

Reinforcement learning in this repository trains control policies by maximizing cumulative reward in simulation. The RL stack uses Isaac Lab environments with SKRL or RSL-RL on Azure ML and OSMO.

## Core Concepts

| Concept | Practical meaning in this toolchain |
| --- | --- |
| Environment | Isaac Lab task such as `Isaac-Velocity-Rough-Anymal-C-v0` |
| Policy | Neural network mapping observations to actions |
| Reward | Scalar objective defining desired behavior quality |
| Rollout | Sequence of transitions gathered from parallel environments |
| Update step | Policy/value optimization using rollout data |

## Why RL Here

| Benefit | Relevance |
| --- | --- |
| Autonomy under uncertainty | Robot behavior can improve without frame-level action labels |
| Scalable simulation | Thousands of parallel environments improve sample throughput |
| Reward-driven optimization | Tasks can optimize outcome quality instead of mimicking demonstrations |

## RL Backends Used

| Backend | Strength | Common use |
| --- | --- | --- |
| SKRL | Broad algorithm coverage and MLflow integration | General-purpose robotics training |
| RSL-RL | Efficient PPO variants for locomotion and distillation | Locomotion-focused workloads |

## Design Considerations

| Area | Guidance |
| --- | --- |
| Reward design | Define sparse and dense terms to avoid reward hacking |
| Simulation fidelity | Match contact, latency, and actuator limits to deployment targets |
| Checkpoint strategy | Use `warm-start` or `resume` modes based on transfer objective |
| Metric selection | Track reward, stability, episode length, and learning dynamics |

## Typical Failure Modes

| Failure mode | Observable signal | Initial mitigation |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Reward hacking | High reward but poor qualitative behavior | Redesign reward terms and constraints |
| Unstable updates | Loss spikes or collapse | Reduce learning rate, adjust clipping, increase rollout horizon |
| Sim-to-real gap | SIL success but poor real-world performance | Improve domain randomization and validation coverage |

## Next Steps

- [Isaac Lab Training](../training/isaac-lab-training.qmd)
- [Experiment Tracking](../training/experiment-tracking.qmd)
- [OSMO Evaluation Workflows](../evaluation/osmo-evaluation.qmd)
